content.problemStatement: Chef likes toys. His favourite toy is an array of length **N**. This array contains only integers. He plays with this array every day. His favourite game with this array is **Segment Multiplication**. In this game, the second player tells the **left** and **right** side of a segment and some **modulo**. The first player should find **the multiplication of all the integers in this segment of the array modulo the given modulus**. Chef is playing this game. Of course, he is the first player and wants to win all the games. To win any game he should write the correct answer for each segment. Although Chef is very clever, he has no time to play games. Hence he asks you to help him. Write the program that solves this problem.
content.inputFormat: First line contains N, the number of elements in the array. Second line contains N integers Ai separated by spaces. Third line contains the number of games T. Each of the next T lines contains 3 integers Li, Ri, and Mi, representing the left side of the segment, the right side of the segment, and the modulo.
content.outputFormat: For each game, output a single line containing the answer for the respective segment, which is the multiplication of all integers in the segment modulo the given modulus.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first game, the segment is [1, 2] and the modulus is 3, so the product is (1 * 2) % 3 = 2. For the second game, the segment is [2, 3] and the modulus is 4, so the product is (2 * 3) % 4 = 2. For the third game, the segment is [1, 1] and the modulus is 1, so the product is (1) % 1 = 0. For the fourth game, the segment is [1, 5] and the modulus is 1000000000, so the product is (1 * 2 * 3 * 4 * 5) % 1000000000 = 120.
For the first game, the segment is [1, 2] and the modulus is 2, so the product is (1 * 2) % 2 = 0. For the second game, the segment is [2, 3] and the modulus is 3, so the product is (2 * 3) % 3 = 0.

For the first game, the segment is [1, 4] and the modulus is 5, so the product is (1 * 2 * 3 * 4) % 5 = 4. For the second game, the segment is [2, 3] and the modulus is 7, so the product is (2 * 3) % 7 = 6. For the third game, the segment is [1, 2] and the modulus is 11, so the product is (1 * 2) % 11 = 2.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over each segment and calculating the product of all integers in the segment modulo the given modulus. This can be done efficiently by using the properties of modular arithmetic.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) per test case, O(T * N) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the input array.

code.Python3:
import sys
N = int(sys.stdin.readline())
arr = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(T):
    Li, Ri, Mi = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
    product = 1
    for i in range(Li - 1, Ri):
        product = (product * arr[i]) % Mi
    print(product)
